title: The Deterministic Horizon: Impossibility Results as Design Specifications for Trustworthy AI Systems
author: arXiv cs.AI
summary: This paper explores how impossibility theorems from fields like social choice and theoretical computer science can serve as design specifications for building trustworthy AI systems. By defining a 'deterministic horizon'—the boundary of what is provably unattainable—the work reframes impossibility results as constructive guardrails, guiding trade-offs in system design to ensure reliability and ethical alignment. The approach offers a principled method to manage constraints in areas such as fairness, privacy, and accountability.
